title: ANH12061 Aortic Aneurysm and Dissection
text_en: Your aorta is divided into two main sections, the thoracic aorta and the abdominal aorta.

title: ANH12061 Aortic Aneurysm and Dissection
text_en: The thoracic aorta has four parts, the aortic root, ascending aorta, aortic arch, and descending aorta.

title: ANH12061 Aortic Aneurysm and Dissection
text_en: The wall of your aorta is composed of three layers that give it strength, the intima or inner layer, the media or middle layer, and the adventisha or outer layer.

title: ANH12061 Aortic Aneurysm and Dissection
text_en: The elastic muscular media prevents your blood pressure from rupturing the wall. Over time, degenerative disease may cause the media layer to break down, weakening the wall of your aorta.

title: ANH12061 Aortic Aneurysm and Dissection
text_en: Blood pumping against the weakened area may cause it to bulge outward like a balloon. When this condition occurs in your chest, it is called a thoracic aortic aneurysm.

title: ANH12061 Aortic Aneurysm and Dissection
text_en: These aneurysms may occur in one of several places, including the aortic root, the ascending aorta, or the descending aorta.

title: ANH12061 Aortic Aneurysm and Dissection
text_en: Most thoracic aneurysms have no symptoms, however, when they reach larger sizes, various symptoms may appear, depending on the severity and location of the aneurysm. Symptoms of stable thoracic aneurysms may include shortness of breath, chest, back, or abdominal pain, difficulty swallowing, and horse-ness.

title: ANH12061 Aortic Aneurysm and Dissection
text_en: If the aneurysm ruptures or dissects, more dramatic symptoms will appear, including a ripping sensation in the chest, severe pain in the back between the shoulder blades, dizziness, and difficulty walking and speaking. If you have these symptoms, you should seek immediate medical attention, as this condition is life-threatening.

title: ANH12061 Aortic Aneurysm and Dissection
text_en: Blood pumping against weakened aortic walls can lead to another life-threatening condition, called aortic dissection, which may or may not occur within an aneurysm. Most commonly, aortic dissection begins with damage to the intima layer. Blood moves through the break in the intima, separating it from the media, causing bleeding inside the wall of your aorta. As a result, a flap called a septum forms between the true aortic channel and the false channel.

title: ANH12061 Aortic Aneurysm and Dissection
text_en: From here, the blood may break through the outer layer of the aortic wall, causing an immediate life-threatening condition, or it may re-enter the aorta through another tear in the intima. Over time, a blood clot may form in the false channel.

title: ANH12061 Aortic Aneurysm and Dissection
text_en: An aortic dissection may be classified, according to its location and duration of symptoms. In the Stanford system, if you have an aortic dissection in your ascending aorta, it is called a type A dissection. All other dissections are called type B.

title: ANH12061 Aortic Aneurysm and Dissection
text_en: The most common symptom of the aortic dissection is sharp or stabbing chest pain, usually sudden and severe. If you have a type A dissection, you're most likely to feel pain in your chest. If you have a type B dissection, you're more likely to feel pain in your back than your chest. If you have these symptoms, you should seek immediate medical attention, as this condition can be life-threatening.

title: ANH12066 Healthcare-Associated Infections
text_en: Health Care Associated Infections or HAIs are infections you may get in a health care facility while you are receiving treatment for a different condition.

title: ANH12066 Healthcare-Associated Infections
text_en: Bacteria such as methamillicillin-resistant staphlococcus aureus, also known as merse, interococcus species, and claustridium difficile or C-diff are frequently the cause of health care associated infections.

title: ANH12066 Healthcare-Associated Infections
text_en: These infections are a major cause of death around the world. One of the most common ways these bacteria get passed from person to person is through lack of proper hand hygiene. When people don't clean their hands, they can transfer microbes from one patient to another, or from one part of the body to another on the same patient.

title: ANH12066 Healthcare-Associated Infections
text_en: There are four common types of health care associated infections.

title: ANH12066 Healthcare-Associated Infections
text_en: A surgical wound infection can result when bacteria get into your incision and spread through your tissues, leading to infection.

title: ANH12066 Healthcare-Associated Infections
text_en: A urinary tract infection can occur when you have a catheter in your bladder, which is a tube used to drain your urine. Bacteria can enter your urinary system on the catheter, enabling the bacteria to travel into your bladder where they can grow.

title: ANH12066 Healthcare-Associated Infections
text_en: A bloodstream infection can occur when you have a central line in your vein, which is a slender tube used to deliver fluids or medications into a large vein near your heart. Bacteria can enter the skin around the insertion site, or directly into your bloodstream, where they can cause infection.

title: ANH12066 Healthcare-Associated Infections
text_en: Numonia can result when you are on a ventilator or breathing machine. Bacteria can enter your throat while you are being placed on the ventilator. Then you may inhale the bacteria into your lungs, leading to infection that can cause pneumonia.

title: ANH12066 Healthcare-Associated Infections
text_en: Hand hygiene, a simple, low-cost and effective procedure, is the single most important method of preventing health care associated infections.

title: ANH12066 Healthcare-Associated Infections
text_en: All people in health care facilities, including patients, visitors and medical personnel, can help prevent these infections by cleaning their hands frequently. Hand hygiene can be performed with soap and running water for 40 to 60 seconds, or by thoroughly rubbing on an alcohol-based hand sanitizer for 20 to 30 seconds. If you are receiving health care, you are part of the health care team, and you should make sure that everyone who comes into contact with you cleans their hands.

title: ANH12068 CT Scan
text_en: A CT scan, also known as a CAT scan or computerized axial tomography, is a painless diagnostic test that uses X-rays and computers to create cross-sectional images of bones and tissues inside

title: ANH12068 CT Scan
text_en: Your doctor may recommend a CT scan to examine your body for any of the following. Ant clots, broken bones, cancerous tumors, infections, internal injuries and bleeding, and signs of heart and vascular disease.

title: ANH12068 CT Scan
text_en: A CT scan helps your doctor select the correct location for surgery, biopsy, or radiation therapy, check the treatment of cancer or heart disease, and check your condition after

title: ANH12068 CT Scan
text_en: A CT scanner is a large square or round X-ray machine with a tunnel through the center.

title: ANH12068 CT Scan
text_en: During your CT scan, you will lie on a table that slowly passes through the tunnel. As you move through the tunnel, a giant ring called a gantry will rotate around your body.

title: ANH12068 CT Scan
text_en: The gantry contains a tube that will release X-ray beams and detectors that will measure the amount of radiation absorbed by your body. The X-ray beams will capture many views of your body from different angles as the gantry

title: ANH12068 CT Scan
text_en: The detectors will send data to a computer that will create cross-sectional images of the bones and soft tissues inside your body.

title: ANH12068 CT Scan
text_en: The scan allows your doctor to see the location of a condition inside your body, which will help them decide how to treat it or to see how well your treatment is progressing.
